# Beads for the universal harness interface refactor.
#
# Governing specs:
#   - docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-014-universal-harness-interface.md
#   - docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-004-harness-implementation.md
#   - docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md
#
# Sequencing note: beads in the 5x, 6x, 7x groups (per-harness migrations)
# can run in parallel with each other once 04 lands, but each group's
# sub-sequence (a → b → c → d → e → f) is strictly ordered. 08, 09 can
# start after 04 lands. 10, 11, 12 are tail-end and depend on prior
# migrations completing.

beads:
  # ----- Phase 0: Baseline and contract scaffolding -----

  - id: BEAD-HARNESS-IF-00
    title: "Pre-refactor baseline: pin CONTRACT-003 JSON fixtures and runtime baseline; grep cmd/ and root for hidden per-harness imports"
    spec_ref: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md
    acceptance_criteria:
      - "testdata/contract-003/pre-refactor/ contains JSON fixtures for HarnessInfo, ProviderInfo, QuotaState, AccountStatus across all five harnesses and one subscription-backed provider"
      - "grep -rEn 'internal/harnesses/(claude|codex|gemini|opencode|pi)' --include='*.go' . (excluding the harness packages, node_modules, .claude/worktrees, benchmark-results, .ddx) is captured verbatim in the PR description; any file not in the plan's Service-side consumers or Tests inventory tables is added"
      - "testdata/perf-baselines/2026-05-14-pre-refactor.txt records go test -count=1 ./... -timeout 30m wall time"
      - "go test ./... -count=1 passes with fixtures committed"
    depends_on: []

  - id: BEAD-HARNESS-IF-01
    title: "Add Harness sub-interfaces and universal types (CONTRACT-004 Step 1)"
    spec_ref: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-004-harness-implementation.md
    acceptance_criteria:
      - "internal/harnesses/types.go declares QuotaHarness, AccountHarness, ModelDiscoveryHarness interfaces with the CONTRACT-004 signatures including SupportedLimitIDs() and SupportedAliases()"
      - "internal/harnesses/types.go declares QuotaStatus, AccountSnapshot, QuotaStateValue, RoutingPreference types and ErrAliasNotResolvable sentinel"
      - "Unit tests cover RoutingPreference enum identity, QuotaStateValue string mapping, sentinel error identity"
      - "No harness implements the new interfaces yet; service code is unchanged; go test ./... passes"
    depends_on:
      - BEAD-HARNESS-IF-00

  - id: BEAD-HARNESS-IF-02
    title: "Conformance suite and synthetic harness implementations"
    spec_ref: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md
    acceptance_criteria:
      - "internal/harnesses/harnesstest package exports RunHarnessConformance, RunQuotaHarnessConformance, RunAccountHarnessConformance, RunModelDiscoveryHarnessConformance"
      - "Synthetic constructors NewSyntheticQuotaHarness, NewSyntheticAccountHarness, NewSyntheticModelDiscoveryHarness accept Supported* values as parameters"
      - "Conformance suite asserts emitted Windows[].LimitID is a subset of SupportedLimitIDs(); asserts ResolveModelAlias covers SupportedAliases() positive path and rejects out-of-set with ErrAliasNotResolvable"
      - "Suite asserts contract shape of single-flight (no error, consistent post-state on concurrent RefreshQuota), not exact probe count"
      - "Smoke test runs synthetic harnesses against the conformance suite and passes"
    depends_on:
      - BEAD-HARNESS-IF-01

  - id: BEAD-HARNESS-IF-03
    title: "CONTRACT-003 projection helpers (projectQuotaStatus, projectAccountSnapshot)"
    spec_ref: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-004-harness-implementation.md
    acceptance_criteria:
      - "service_projection.go (or equivalent) exports projectQuotaStatus(harnesses.QuotaStatus) *QuotaState and projectAccountSnapshot(harnesses.AccountSnapshot) *AccountStatus"
      - "Helpers drive every QuotaStateValue, every RoutingPreference, populated and empty Account/Detail, present and absent Reason in unit tests"
      - "Tests assert public JSON shape matches BEAD-HARNESS-IF-00 fixtures structurally"
      - "No service code calls the helpers yet — they exist for Phase 5+"
    depends_on:
      - BEAD-HARNESS-IF-02

  - id: BEAD-HARNESS-IF-04
    title: "Async refresh scheduler and harnessByName helper"
    spec_ref: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md
    acceptance_criteria:
      - "Service struct gains a harnessByName(name string) harnesses.Harness method consulting the registered-harnesses field"
      - "service_refresh_scheduler.go owns a debounced ticker per registered QuotaHarness firing at QuotaFreshness()/2 and per AccountHarness whose AccountFreshness() differs from quota"
      - "Scheduler discovers harnesses by iterating service.harnessByName over the registry and type-asserting to QuotaHarness / AccountHarness independently"
      - "Scheduler reads QuotaStatus before each potential refresh to obtain CapturedAt; on cache-cold (State=QuotaUnavailable) the scheduler always calls RefreshQuota"
      - "Scheduler skips a tick when last-known QuotaStatus.CapturedAt is within QuotaFreshness() of now (cache-freshness dedup is the scheduler's job, not RefreshQuota's)"
      - "Startup refresh kickoff fires once on scheduler construction for every registered QuotaHarness / AccountHarness with a cache-cold state, per the cache-cold path in primary-harness-capability-baseline.md"
      - "Fake-clock tests prove debounce cadence, single-flight respect via cache lock, startup kickoff, and clean ctx cancel"
      - "Per-harness Refresh*Async helpers remain in place for now (deleted in each harness's e-step)"
    depends_on:
      - BEAD-HARNESS-IF-02

  # ----- Phase 5: Claude migration sub-sequence (strictly ordered) -----

  - id: BEAD-HARNESS-IF-05A
    title: "claude.Runner: add CONTRACT-004 interface methods (additive; old surface still present)"
    spec_ref: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md
    acceptance_criteria:
      - "claude.Runner implements QuotaStatus, RefreshQuota, QuotaFreshness, SupportedLimitIDs, AccountStatus, RefreshAccount, AccountFreshness, DefaultModelSnapshot, ResolveModelAlias, SupportedAliases"
      - "RefreshQuota probes unconditionally and uses the existing cache lock for single-flight (queued callers consume in-flight result)"
      - "Routing-preference mapping helper translates today's PreferClaude+freshness into RoutingPreference"
      - "package claude doc comment (doc.go or runner.go top) mirrors SupportedLimitIDs() and SupportedAliases() return values"
      - "harnesstest.RunHarnessConformance, RunQuotaHarnessConformance, RunAccountHarnessConformance, RunModelDiscoveryHarnessConformance all pass against &claude.Runner{}"
      - "Old per-harness cache I/O functions still exported and unchanged; both surfaces coexist"
    depends_on:
      - BEAD-HARNESS-IF-04

  - id: BEAD-HARNESS-IF-05B
    title: "Migrate service.go claude consumers to QuotaHarness/AccountHarness interface"
    spec_ref: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md
    acceptance_criteria:
      - "service.go drops the claudeharness import"
      - "Every claude-related call in service.go fetches the runner via harnessByName, type-asserts to the sub-interface, and projects through service_projection helpers"
      - "go test ./... passes; BEAD-HARNESS-IF-00 fixtures pass structural diff for claude rows"
    depends_on:
      - BEAD-HARNESS-IF-05A
      - BEAD-HARNESS-IF-03

  - id: BEAD-HARNESS-IF-05C
    title: "Migrate service_providers.go claude consumers"
    spec_ref: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md
    acceptance_criteria:
      - "service_providers.go drops claudeharness imports for claude paths"
      - "ClaudeQuotaSnapshot{} construction at service_providers.go:620 moves inside claude.Runner.RefreshQuota"
      - "Health-check callbacks become qh.RefreshQuota(ctx) calls"
      - "go test ./... passes; structural-diff fixtures pass"
    depends_on:
      - BEAD-HARNESS-IF-05B

  - id: BEAD-HARNESS-IF-05D
    title: "Migrate service_models.go and service_subscription_quota.go claude consumers"
    spec_ref: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md
    acceptance_criteria:
      - "service_models.go uses ModelDiscoveryHarness for claude alias resolution"
      - "service_subscription_quota.go consumes QuotaStatus.RoutingPreference; claudeQuotaMaxUsedPercent derives over QuotaStatus.Windows uniformly across claude and codex"
      - "Type assertion on *claudeharness.ClaudeQuotaSnapshot at service_subscription_quota.go:79 is removed"
      - "go test ./... passes; structural-diff fixtures pass"
    depends_on:
      - BEAD-HARNESS-IF-05C

  - id: BEAD-HARNESS-IF-05E
    title: "Delete and unexport claude public surface"
    spec_ref: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md
    acceptance_criteria:
      - "ClaudeQuotaRoutingDecision struct, DecideClaudeQuotaRouting, ReadClaudeQuotaRoutingDecision deleted"
      - "ClaudeQuotaSnapshot lowercased to claudeQuotaSnapshot (package-private)"
      - "ReadClaudeQuota, ReadClaudeQuotaFrom, WriteClaudeQuota, ClaudeQuotaCachePath, IsClaudeQuotaFresh, ClaudeQuotaSnapshotAge, DefaultClaudeQuotaStaleAfter unexported"
      - "ReadClaudeQuotaViaPTY, RefreshClaudeQuotaViaPTY, ReadClaudeQuotaFromCassette, DefaultClaudeModelDiscovery, ResolveClaudeFamilyAlias, ReadClaudeReasoningFromHelp, IsClaudeQuotaExhaustedMessage, MarkClaudeQuotaExhaustedFromMessage unexported"
      - "RefreshClaudeQuotaAsync deleted; the Step 4 scheduler now drives claude refresh cadence"
      - "Pre-merge check: reverting only this bead (without reverting 05A–05D) leaves the tree compiling and tests green"
      - "go test ./... passes; structural-diff fixtures pass"
    depends_on:
      - BEAD-HARNESS-IF-05D

  - id: BEAD-HARNESS-IF-05F
    title: "Migrate claude-related tests to interface mocks and fixture cache files"
    spec_ref: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md
    acceptance_criteria:
      - "service_status_test.go, service_subscription_quota_test.go, service_routing_errors_test.go, service_providers_test.go, service_routing_test.go, harness_golden_integration_test.go no longer reference claudeharness exports beyond the dispatcher constructor"
      - "internal/harnesses/claude/*_test.go files run as package claude (white-box) where they need unexported helpers"
      - "go test ./... passes; runtime budget maintained"
    depends_on:
      - BEAD-HARNESS-IF-05E

1	# Implementation Plan: Universal Harness Interface Refactor
2	
3	**Date**: 2026-05-14
4	**Status**: DRAFT
5	**Governs**: [ADR-014](./adr/ADR-014-universal-harness-interface.md), [CONTRACT-004](./contracts/CONTRACT-004-harness-implementation.md)
6	**Supersedes scope of**: ADR-013 (withdrawn pending this refactor)
7	**Rough size**: 12 numbered steps totaling ~20–25 PRs over 6–10 weeks for one engineer serialized (less with parallelism across the per-harness migrations after Step 4 lands); Steps 5/6/7 each land as 4–6 sub-PRs
8	
9	## Problem Statement
10	
11	The service depends on roughly 80 exported symbols across the per-harness
12	packages beyond the documented `Harness` interface — quota cache I/O,
13	routing-decision functions, concrete snapshot types with field-level
14	reads, family-alias resolvers, PTY probe entry points, and account-file
15	readers. A 2026-05-14 inventory counted **69 external call sites** across
16	six service files. The `Harness` interface alone does not capture the
17	contract; adding any new harness today requires either duplicating ~25
18	symbols under a new prefix or rewiring service code per-harness.
19	
20	CONTRACT-004 defines the universal interface. This plan ports every
21	existing harness, every service consumer, every test, and the
22	public-facing CONTRACT-003 projection onto that contract, then enforces
23	the boundary with a lint rule.
24	
25	## Requirements
26	
27	### Functional
28	
29	- Every existing harness implements the CONTRACT-004 interfaces it
30	  qualifies for: `Harness` (all), `QuotaHarness` (claude, codex,
31	  gemini), `AccountHarness` (gemini; optional for claude/codex),
32	  `ModelDiscoveryHarness` (all five).
33	- Service-side code reads quota, routing preference, account state,
34	  and model discovery exclusively through the interfaces. No file in
35	  `service*.go`, `internal/serviceimpl/`, `internal/runtimesignals/`,
36	  or `cmd/` imports per-harness package symbols beyond the runner
37	  constructor used by the dispatcher.
38	- Per-harness `*QuotaSnapshot`, `*QuotaRoutingDecision`,
39	  `*QuotaCachePath`, `Read*Quota*`, `Write*Quota*`,
40	  `Default*QuotaStaleAfter`, `Decide*QuotaRouting`,
41	  `Default*ModelDiscovery`, and `Resolve*Alias` symbols are removed
42	  from the public surface (deleted or unexported).
43	- Public CONTRACT-003 JSON shapes (`HarnessInfo`, `ProviderInfo`,
44	  `QuotaState`, `AccountStatus`) remain structurally identical (same
45	  field set, same value semantics) to pre-refactor recorded fixtures.
46	- The service has a single async refresh scheduler that consumes
47	  `QuotaHarness.QuotaFreshness()` and `AccountHarness.AccountFreshness()`
48	  to drive refreshes, replacing per-harness `Refresh*Async` functions.
49	
50	### Non-Functional
51	
52	- Refactor is staged one harness at a time so regressions surface
53	  early.
54	- The lint rule that enforces the boundary lands in the last step;
55	  partial migrations cannot accidentally satisfy it.
56	- No record-mode cassette is invalidated; cache file on-disk JSON
57	  shapes remain stable (cache file schemas use field-tagged JSON,
58	  not type-name encoding).
59	- Pre-existing test suite runtime does not regress by more than 10%
60	  (measured as `go test -count=1 ./... -timeout 30m` wall time before
61	  Step 0 and after the final step, excluding new test packages
62	  introduced by Steps 2 and 4). No individual pre-existing test slows
63	  by more than 10%. New conformance and scheduler tests are budgeted
64	  and reviewed separately in the Step 11 PR.

558	## Test Strategy
559	
560	| Layer | Coverage |
561	|-------|----------|
562	| Unit (per harness) | Interface method implementations; routing-preference mapping; cache I/O against synthetic files. |
563	| Conformance suite | Every harness runs Step 2 assertions for its declared interfaces. Suite tests itself against the synthetic harness in Step 2. |
564	| Service-level | JSON-shape preservation against Step 0 fixtures via structural diff in Step 11; routing decisions against synthetic `QuotaHarness` implementations covering every `RoutingPreference` × `QuotaStateValue` combination. |
565	| Integration | Existing `harness_golden_integration_test.go` cassettes continue to pass without modification (cassettes are below the interface boundary). |
566	| Concurrency | The conformance suite asserts `RefreshQuota` and `RefreshAccount` single-flight under concurrent invocation (CONTRACT-004 invariant #7). |
567	| Lint | Step 10 boundary rule runs in CI. |
568	| Regression | Pre-existing test suite within 10% of Step 0 baseline (asserted in Step 11); no individual pre-existing test slower by more than 10%; new conformance + scheduler tests budgeted separately. |
569	
570	## Rollback Strategy
571	
572	Each step's PR is independently revertable.
573	
574	- **Cache file compatibility**: All per-harness cache files use
575	  field-tagged JSON encoding. Renaming the Go type from
576	  `ClaudeQuotaSnapshot` to `claudeQuotaSnapshot` does not change the
577	  on-disk JSON shape (verified for each harness in Step 5a/6a/7a
578	  before unexporting). A revert of Step 5e reintroduces the exported
579	  type name without invalidating cached files written under the new
580	  code.
581	- **Per-harness rollback**: Reverting Step 5 (claude) does not require
582	  reverting Steps 6–8 (codex/gemini/opencode/pi) because each
583	  harness's interface implementation is independent. The reverted
584	  harness goes back to its per-harness public surface; the others
585	  remain on the interface.
586	- **Pre-lint coexistence**: Step 10's lint rule lands last
587	  intentionally so that partial-state environments (some harnesses
588	  migrated, some not) compile and test. A revert during this window
589	  produces a working tree; a revert after Step 10 may require
590	  reverting Step 10 alongside.
591	- **Public JSON shape**: Step 11's structural-diff assertion is the
592	  guarantee that any merged state preserves CONTRACT-003. A diff at
593	  Step 11 blocks merge; rollback is a revert of the offending step,
594	  not a forward-fix on a broken contract.
595	
596	## Risks and Mitigations
597	
598	| Risk | Mitigation |
599	|------|------------|
600	| JSON shape regression during projection refactor | Step 0 fixtures pinned before any refactor work; Step 11 asserts structural equality with documented time-field handling. |
601	| Routing semantics drift (boolean `PreferX` → `RoutingPreference` enum) | Step 5 (claude) lands fully and runs the routing test suite before Step 6 (codex) begins. Each subsequent harness re-runs the suite. |
602	| Cache file schema drift when snapshots are renamed | Cache files use field-tagged JSON; type renames don't affect on-disk shape. Each Step 5a/6a/7a includes a load-old-write-new round-trip test before unexporting. |
603	| Conformance suite is too strict and blocks legitimate variation | Conformance covers only CONTRACT-004 contract behavior. Harness-specific tests stay in the harness package. The suite is itself tested in Step 2 against the synthetic harness. |
604	| Lint exemption file accumulates as a backdoor | The exemption list in Step 10 is one line; growing it requires an ADR amendment. |
605	| Refactor stalls partway, leaving mixed surface | Step 10 (lint rule) lands last; partial state is a valid intermediate. Each harness migration's sub-sequence (5e/6e/7e/8) deletes the old surface as an explicit acceptance gate before the next harness starts. |
606	| `claude-tui` work resurfaces during the refactor and bypasses it | ADR-013 is withdrawn (not paused); re-proposal must cite the merged CONTRACT-004. |
607	| Async refresh scheduler is wrong and refreshes too often or too rarely | Step 4's scheduler tests use a fake clock and assert exact tick counts per `QuotaFreshness()` value. Production scheduling defects are caught by `service_status` integration tests measuring actual refresh cadence. |
608	| Step 5 sub-sequence stalls partway (5b lands, 5c does not) | Intermediate states (5a-5d) are valid: claude's new interface methods coexist with the old surface. The old surface only disappears in 5e. |
609	
610	## Acceptance Criteria
611	
612	1. `internal/harnesses/types.go` declares the four interfaces and
613	   universal types per CONTRACT-004.
614	2. Every existing harness implements the interfaces appropriate to it
615	   (verified by the Step 2 conformance suite).
616	3. No `.go` file outside `internal/harnesses/` imports a symbol from
617	   `internal/harnesses/<name>/` except
618	   `internal/serviceimpl/execute_dispatch.go`.
619	4. Per-harness `*QuotaSnapshot` types are lowercase.
620	5. Per-harness `*QuotaRoutingDecision` types are deleted.
621	6. Per-harness cache I/O functions are unexported.
622	7. The async refresh scheduler from Step 4 owns all per-harness
623	   refresh cadence; per-harness `Refresh*Async` helpers are deleted.
624	8. CONTRACT-003 JSON fixtures (Step 0) pass structural diff
625	   post-refactor (Step 11).
626	9. Lint rule from Step 10 runs in CI and blocks new forbidden imports.
627	10. PRD, CONTRACT-003, and AGENTS.md cross-references are updated.
628	11. Pre-existing test suite runtime is within 10% of the Step 0
629	    baseline (excluding new packages from Steps 2 and 4); no
630	    individual pre-existing test slows by more than 10%.

632	## Follow-Ups (Out of Scope)
633	
634	- **`claude-tui` re-proposal.** Once this refactor lands, ADR-013
635	  may be re-proposed citing CONTRACT-004. The fork then becomes
636	  additive: a new `internal/harnesses/claude-tui/` package
637	  implementing the same four interfaces, sharing a snapshot type with
638	  `claude` through an `internal/harnesses/anthropic/` neutral
639	  subpackage (since both snapshots are now package-private, sharing
640	  them requires only moving the type into a shared internal
641	  package).
642	
643	  ADR-013's withdrawal note records design constraints for the
644	  re-proposal — prior art surveyed (claude-p, shannon),
645	  transcript-JSONL-as-evidence convergence, hooks-driven transport
646	  via `--settings '<inline-json>'`, pooled long-lived sessions with
647	  `/clear` between turns bounded by the fiz process lifetime, pool
648	  key per `(harness, workdir)` at package or service scope (not on
649	  the Runner struct), startup orphan reaper analogous to the
650	  existing `service_stale_harness_reaper*.go`, hook-conflict
651	  handling against operator `~/.claude/settings.json`, and the
652	  pre-implementation gate that verifies `/clear` semantics against
653	  the installed Claude Code version. Re-proposal still requires
654	  empirical evidence that PTY+hooks-driven Claude moves the
655	  `/usage` window — the structural refactor here does not validate
656	  that premise. CONTRACT-004 and ADR-014 are transport-agnostic and
657	  unaffected by these constraints; they live below the interface.
658	- **`HarnessConfig` registry cleanup.** The current `HarnessConfig`
659	  struct mixes interface-relevant metadata with subprocess-specific
660	  knobs. Splitting these is a separate cleanup; CONTRACT-004 does
661	  not require it.
662	- **Provider-side interface.** HTTP providers (`openrouter`,
663	  `lmstudio`, `omlx`) have their own per-provider exports. A
664	  parallel `ProviderHarness` contract is plausible but not in scope.

205	// HarnessInfo type defined in CONTRACT-003. Internal callers use this to
206	// implement the public ListHarnesses surface without re-declaring the shape.
207	type HarnessInfo struct {
208		Name                 string
209		Type                 string // "native" | "subprocess"
210		Available            bool
211		Path                 string
212		Error                string
213		IsLocal              bool
214		IsSubscription       bool
215		AutoRoutingEligible  bool
216		ExactPinSupport      bool
217		DefaultModel         string
218		SupportedPermissions []string
219		SupportedReasoning   []string
220		CostClass            string
221	}
222	
223	// ExecuteRequest is the internal request carried into Harness.Execute. It
224	// is intentionally narrower than the public ExecuteRequest in CONTRACT-003:
225	// the agent's routing layer is expected to resolve provider/model/reasoning
226	// /permissions/timeouts before invoking a harness, so the harness sees a
227	// concrete, ready-to-run request.
228	type ExecuteRequest struct {
229		// Prompt is the resolved user prompt sent to the model.
230		Prompt string
231	
232		// SystemPrompt is the resolved system prompt; empty means harness default.
233		SystemPrompt string
234	
235		// Provider is the resolved provider identifier when applicable. May be
236		// empty for harnesses that have no provider concept (e.g. claude CLI).
237		Provider string
238	
239		// Model is the resolved model identifier; empty means harness default.
240		Model string
241	
242		// WorkDir is the working directory for tool operations. Required when
243		// the chosen harness uses tools.
244		WorkDir string
245	
246		// Permissions is "safe" | "supervised" | "unrestricted". Empty defaults to "safe".
247		Permissions string
248	
249		// Temperature is the model sampling temperature requested by the caller.
250		// Harness adapters may ignore it when their CLI has no equivalent control.
251		Temperature float32
252	
253		// Seed is the requested sampling seed. Zero means unset/provider chooses.
254		// Harness adapters may ignore it when their CLI has no equivalent control.
255		Seed int64
256	
257		// Reasoning is the normalized public reasoning scalar. Empty/off means no
258		// adapter flag should be emitted.
259		Reasoning string
260	
261		// Timeout is the wall-clock cap for the entire request. 0 disables.
262		Timeout time.Duration
263	
264		// IdleTimeout is the streaming-quiet cap. 0 uses harness default.
265		IdleTimeout time.Duration
266	
267		// SessionLogDir overrides the per-run session-log directory; harness
268		// uses this to direct progress traces into a per-bundle evidence dir.
269		SessionLogDir string
270	
271		// SessionID is a stable identifier for the run, used in progress log
272		// filenames and event metadata. Empty means the harness generates one.
273		SessionID string
274	
275		// Metadata is echoed back into Event.Metadata (e.g. bead_id, attempt_id).
276		Metadata map[string]string
277	}
278	
279	// Harness is the internal contract every harness implementation in
280	// internal/harnesses/<name> satisfies. It is the minimal surface the agent
281	// dispatcher needs to route a resolved request into a backend.
282	//
283	// A Harness is responsible for emitting events on the returned channel until
284	// execution completes; the channel MUST be closed after the final event so
285	// downstream consumers can detect end-of-stream. The final event is always
286	// of type EventTypeFinal.
287	type Harness interface {
288		// Info returns identity + capability metadata for this harness.
289		Info() HarnessInfo
290	
291		// HealthCheck triggers a fresh probe (binary present, auth ok, etc.)
292		// and returns nil if the harness is ready to execute.
293		HealthCheck(ctx context.Context) error
294	
295		// Execute runs one resolved request. Events stream on the returned
296		// channel; a single final event closes the stream. The first error
297		// return is reserved for setup failures (binary missing, etc.) — once
298		// the channel is returned, all per-run failures are reported via a
299		// final event with Status != "success".
300		Execute(ctx context.Context, req ExecuteRequest) (<-chan Event, error)
301	}
302	
303	// QuotaStateValue is the normalized state enumeration consumed by
304	// CONTRACT-004 sub-interfaces. Only QuotaOK and QuotaStale carry
305	// routing-usable signal; other values MUST NOT result in
306	// RoutingPreferenceAvailable.
307	type QuotaStateValue string
308	
309	const (
310		QuotaOK              QuotaStateValue = "ok"
311		QuotaStale           QuotaStateValue = "stale"
312		QuotaBlocked         QuotaStateValue = "blocked"
313		QuotaUnavailable     QuotaStateValue = "unavailable"
314		QuotaUnauthenticated QuotaStateValue = "unauthenticated"
315		QuotaUnknown         QuotaStateValue = "unknown"
316	)
317	
318	// RoutingPreference is the routing layer's consumable signal indicating
319	// whether a harness should be preferred given its current quota evidence.
320	// It is an internal routing signal — never projected into the public
321	// CONTRACT-003 surface.
322	type RoutingPreference int
323	
324	const (
325		RoutingPreferenceUnknown RoutingPreference = iota
326		RoutingPreferenceAvailable
327		RoutingPreferenceBlocked
328	)
329	
330	// QuotaStatus is the universal quota report defined by CONTRACT-004.
331	// Each harness's private snapshot type projects into this; the private
332	// snapshot is never exposed across package boundaries.
333	type QuotaStatus struct {
334		// Source identifies how the underlying evidence was captured:
335		// "pty", "cache", "session-token-count", "cli", "api".
336		Source string
337	
338		// CapturedAt is when the underlying evidence was observed (not when
339		// this status struct was assembled).
340		CapturedAt time.Time
341	
342		// Fresh reports whether CapturedAt is within QuotaFreshness() at the
343		// time of the call.
344		Fresh bool
345	
346		// Age is now - CapturedAt at the time of the call.
347		Age time.Duration
348	
349		// State is the normalized state. Only QuotaOK and QuotaStale carry
350		// routing-usable signal.
351		State QuotaStateValue
352	
353		// Windows captures per-window evidence (5h, weekly, tier-specific).
354		// Authoritative for any structured fact the routing layer or
355		// operator surfaces consume — including tier breakdowns.
356		Windows []QuotaWindow
357	
358		// Account is the account/plan/auth evidence captured alongside
359		// quota. Nil when the harness has no concept of account or when
360		// account evidence is delivered through AccountHarness only.
361		Account *AccountSnapshot
362	
363		// RoutingPreference indicates whether the routing layer should
364		// prefer this harness given the current evidence.
365		RoutingPreference RoutingPreference
366	
367		// Reason is a short human-readable explanation of State and
368		// RoutingPreference — surfaced in operator views and routing logs.
369		Reason string
370	
371		// Detail is harness-specific opaque metadata for diagnostic display
372		// only. Service code MAY surface it verbatim in operator views;
373		// service code MUST NOT branch on its keys or values for routing
374		// decisions.
375		Detail map[string]string
376	}
377	
378	// AccountSnapshot is the universal account/auth report defined by
379	// CONTRACT-004. Projects onto the public AccountStatus type defined in
380	// CONTRACT-003.
381	type AccountSnapshot struct {
382		Authenticated   bool
383		Unauthenticated bool
384		Email           string
385		PlanType        string
386		OrgName         string
387		Source          string // file path, env var name, "cache", "cli"
388		CapturedAt      time.Time
389		Fresh           bool
390		Detail          string // free-form diagnostic detail
391	}
392	
393	// ErrAliasNotResolvable is returned by ModelDiscoveryHarness.ResolveModelAlias
394	// when the requested family is not recognized or the supplied discovery
395	// snapshot has no matching concrete model.
396	var ErrAliasNotResolvable = errors.New("model alias not resolvable from snapshot")
397	
398	// QuotaHarness is implemented by harnesses that own a subscription or
399	// quota window. See CONTRACT-004 for the full normative contract.
400	type QuotaHarness interface {
401		Harness
402	
403		// QuotaStatus returns the current quota state from the harness's
404		// owned cache, with Fresh/Age computed against now. MUST be cheap
405		// (no live probe) and safe to call on every routing decision.
406		// Absence of evidence is reported via State=QuotaUnavailable on a
407		// valid QuotaStatus value; the error return is reserved for call
408		// failure (ctx cancelled, IO failure, lock acquisition failure).
409		QuotaStatus(ctx context.Context, now time.Time) (QuotaStatus, error)
410	
411		// RefreshQuota drives the harness's live probe, persists the
412		// result through the harness's owned cache, and returns the
413		// resulting status. Single-flight per harness instance via the
414		// harness's cache lock; concurrent callers block. Probe failure
415		// is reported as a QuotaStatus with State=QuotaUnavailable (or
416		// QuotaUnauthenticated for auth-related failures), not as an
417		// error. The error return is reserved for call failure.
418		RefreshQuota(ctx context.Context) (QuotaStatus, error)
419	
420		// QuotaFreshness returns the harness's freshness window (e.g. 15m).
421		// Constant for the harness; cheap to call.
422		QuotaFreshness() time.Duration
423	
424		// SupportedLimitIDs returns the harness's stable set of emitted
425		// Windows[].LimitID values. Constant for the harness; the
426		// conformance suite reads this value to verify that emitted
427		// Windows[].LimitID strings are a subset of this set. Empty
428		// slice is allowed for harnesses that emit no windows.
429		SupportedLimitIDs() []string
430	}
431	
432	// AccountHarness is implemented by harnesses that expose authentication
433	// or account state independent of quota. See CONTRACT-004 for the full
434	// normative contract.
435	type AccountHarness interface {
436		Harness
437	
438		// AccountStatus returns the harness's current account/auth state.
439		// Cheap; reads cached evidence only. Absence of evidence is
440		// reported via AccountSnapshot fields on a valid snapshot; the
441		// error return is reserved for call failure.
442		AccountStatus(ctx context.Context, now time.Time) (AccountSnapshot, error)
443	
444		// RefreshAccount drives the harness's account probe and persists
445		// the result. Single-flight per harness instance; concurrent
446		// callers block. Probe failure is reported via AccountSnapshot
447		// fields, not as an error.
448		RefreshAccount(ctx context.Context) (AccountSnapshot, error)
449	
450		// AccountFreshness returns the harness's account freshness window
451		// (e.g. 7 days for gemini). Constant for the harness; cheap.
452		AccountFreshness() time.Duration
453	}
454	
455	// ModelDiscoveryHarness is implemented by harnesses whose model surface
456	// extends beyond a single Info().DefaultModel — i.e. they support family
457	// aliases (sonnet, gpt, gemini) that resolve through discovery evidence.
458	// See CONTRACT-004 for the full normative contract.
459	type ModelDiscoveryHarness interface {
460		Harness
461	
462		// DefaultModelSnapshot returns the harness's seed/fallback
463		// discovery snapshot. Used to bootstrap the catalog before the
464		// first live refresh lands. Stable for the harness; cheap.
465		DefaultModelSnapshot() ModelDiscoverySnapshot
466	
467		// ResolveModelAlias maps a family-style requested model to a
468		// concrete model ID using the provided discovery snapshot.
469		// Returns ErrAliasNotResolvable if the family is not recognized
470		// or the snapshot has no matching concrete model.
471		ResolveModelAlias(family string, snapshot ModelDiscoverySnapshot) (string, error)
472	
473		// SupportedAliases returns the harness's stable set of family
474		// aliases ResolveModelAlias recognizes. Constant for the harness;
475		// the conformance suite uses this value to verify
476		// ResolveModelAlias covers each documented family (positive path)
477		// and rejects out-of-set families with ErrAliasNotResolvable
478		// (negative path). Empty slice is allowed for harnesses that
479		// recognize no family aliases.
480		SupportedAliases() []string
481	}

9	// projectQuotaStatus projects a CONTRACT-004 harnesses.QuotaStatus onto the
10	// public CONTRACT-003 QuotaState surface per the projection table in
11	// docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-004-harness-implementation.md.
12	//
13	// The helper covers the success path only: it converts a successfully
14	// returned QuotaStatus value. Call failure (a non-nil error from the
15	// harness method) is the caller's responsibility — they own LastError
16	// population because only they know which probe failed. State-driven
17	// absence such as State=QuotaUnavailable is reported via Status, not
18	// LastError, exactly as the projection table specifies.
19	//
20	// QuotaStatus.RoutingPreference is intentionally not projected: it is an
21	// internal routing signal that never reaches the public surface (see
22	// CONTRACT-004 §"Projection to CONTRACT-003" and invariant #4).
23	//
24	// No service code calls this helper yet; the per-harness migrations in
25	// BEAD-HARNESS-IF-05B+ will wire it in.
26	func projectQuotaStatus(status harnesses.QuotaStatus) *QuotaState {
27		state := &QuotaState{
28			Windows:    status.Windows,
29			CapturedAt: status.CapturedAt,
30			Fresh:      status.Fresh,
31			Source:     status.Source,
32			Status:     string(status.State),
33		}
34		if status.Reason != "" {
35			if state.Status == "" {
36				state.Status = status.Reason
37			} else {
38				state.Status = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", state.Status, status.Reason)
39			}
40		}
41		return state
42	}
43

45	// onto the public CONTRACT-003 AccountStatus surface. Every public field
46	// maps 1:1 from the snapshot, including the unknown-state convention
47	// (Authenticated=false && Unauthenticated=false) and the free-form Detail
48	// string. No service code calls this helper yet.
49	func projectAccountSnapshot(snapshot harnesses.AccountSnapshot) *AccountStatus {
50		return &AccountStatus{

8	# CONTRACT-004: Harness Implementation Contract
9	
10	| Field | Value |
11	|-------|-------|
12	| Status | Draft |
13	| Date | 2026-05-14 |
14	| Scope | Internal interface every Fizeau harness package implements; service-side consumers depend only on this contract, never on harness-specific exports. |
15	| Companion | CONTRACT-003 (service-facing API); ADR-014 (decision rationale) |
16	
17	## Purpose
18	
19	CONTRACT-003 specifies the public Fizeau service surface. It does not specify
20	the internal contract that each harness implementation
21	(`internal/harnesses/<name>/`) satisfies. Today that internal contract is the
22	3-method `harnesses.Harness` interface plus an ad-hoc collection of
23	per-harness exported symbols (~80 of them across claude, codex, gemini)
24	that the service imports directly: `ClaudeQuotaSnapshot`,
25	`ReadClaudeQuota`, `DecideClaudeQuotaRouting`, `CodexAuthPath`,
26	`ReadAuthEvidence`, `ResolveClaudeFamilyAlias`, and so on.
27	
28	This contract closes that gap. It defines the complete normative interface
29	every harness implementation MUST satisfy and constrains the service to
30	consume only that interface. Per-harness symbol leakage is the failure
31	mode CONTRACT-004 exists to prevent.
32	
33	## Scope
34	
35	In scope:
36	
37	- The Go interface set every `internal/harnesses/<name>/` package
38	  implements.
39	- The universal types those interfaces return.
40	- Cache and refresh ownership.
41	- Conformance evidence requirements.
42	- Projection rules from internal harness types to the CONTRACT-003
43	  public types (`QuotaState`, `AccountStatus`, `HarnessInfo`,
44	  `ProviderInfo`).
45	
46	Out of scope:
47	
48	- The CLI/network behavior of any specific harness binary (covered by
49	  primary-harness-capability-baseline.md and per-harness specs).
50	- The cassette/replay transport contract (ADR-002).
51	- The public service contract (CONTRACT-003).
52	
53	## Interface Set
54	
55	Every harness implementation MUST implement `Harness`. It MAY additionally
56	implement any of `QuotaHarness`, `AccountHarness`, and
57	`ModelDiscoveryHarness`. The service uses Go interface assertions to
58	discover which optional contracts a harness satisfies and consumes them
59	through the interface only.
60	
61	```go
62	package harnesses
63	
64	// Harness is the minimum every harness implements.
65	type Harness interface {
66	    // Info returns identity + capability metadata. Stable across the
67	    // process lifetime; cheap to call from request hot paths.
68	    Info() HarnessInfo
69	
70	    // HealthCheck triggers a fresh, lightweight readiness probe (binary
71	    // present and executable; non-blocking auth state read where
72	    // possible). Returns nil when ready. MUST NOT drive interactive
73	    // sessions or block on network.
74	    HealthCheck(ctx context.Context) error
75	
76	    // Execute runs one resolved request and returns an event channel.
77	    // The channel emits zero or more progress/text/tool events followed
78	    // by exactly one EventTypeFinal event, then closes. Setup failures
79	    // (binary missing, PTY allocation failure) return as the second
80	    // return value; per-run failures (auth, quota, parser desync,
81	    // timeout, cancellation) MUST be reported via the final event with
82	    // Status != "success".
83	    Execute(ctx context.Context, req ExecuteRequest) (<-chan Event, error)
84	}
85	
86	// QuotaHarness is implemented by harnesses that own a subscription or
87	// quota window. claude, codex, gemini implement; opencode, pi do not.
88	type QuotaHarness interface {
89	    Harness
90	
91	    // QuotaStatus returns the current quota state from the harness's
92	    // owned cache, with Fresh/Age computed against `now`. MUST be cheap
93	    // (no live probe) and safe to call on every routing decision.
94	    // Absence of evidence is reported via State=QuotaUnavailable on a
95	    // valid QuotaStatus value; the error return is reserved for call
96	    // failure (ctx cancelled, IO failure, lock acquisition failure).
97	    QuotaStatus(ctx context.Context, now time.Time) (QuotaStatus, error)
98	
99	    // RefreshQuota drives the harness's live probe (PTY, CLI, or other
100	    // owned transport), persists the result through the harness's owned
101	    // cache, and returns the resulting status. RefreshQuota probes
102	    // unconditionally on every successful lock acquisition; it does
103	    // NOT skip the probe based on cache freshness. Cache-freshness
104	    // deduplication is the caller's responsibility: callers that want
105	    // a possibly-stale-but-cheap read use QuotaStatus; callers that
106	    // orchestrate refresh cadence (e.g. a periodic scheduler) skip
107	    // ticks when the last-known CapturedAt is within QuotaFreshness()
108	    // of the current time. Callers that want a fresh probe call
109	    // RefreshQuota and accept the probe cost.
110	    //
111	    // RefreshQuota is single-flight per harness instance: concurrent
112	    // callers block on the harness's cache lock (per ADR-012). Queued
113	    // callers MUST NOT initiate a second probe — once the in-flight
114	    // refresh completes and releases the lock, queued callers read the
115	    // just-written cached state and return it. The contract guarantee
116	    // is "one probe per single-flight cohort," not "one probe per
117	    // call." Probe failure is reported as a QuotaStatus with
118	    // State=QuotaUnavailable (or QuotaUnauthenticated when the probe
119	    // identifies the failure as auth-related), not as an error. The
120	    // error return is reserved for call failure: ctx cancelled before
121	    // lock acquisition, lock acquisition timeout under deadline,
122	    // unrecoverable lock-file corruption, or unrecoverable IO failure.
123	    // Lock contention itself is not a failure — the caller waits.
124	    RefreshQuota(ctx context.Context) (QuotaStatus, error)
125	
126	    // QuotaFreshness returns the harness's freshness window (e.g. 15m).
127	    // Service code uses this for stale-cache scheduling. Constant for
128	    // the harness; cheap to call.
129	    QuotaFreshness() time.Duration
130	
131	    // SupportedLimitIDs returns the harness's stable set of emitted
132	    // Windows[].LimitID values (e.g. "session", "weekly-all",
133	    // "weekly-sonnet" for claude; "tier-flash", "tier-pro" for
134	    // gemini). Constant for the harness; the harness's package doc
135	    // also enumerates this set for human readers. The conformance
136	    // suite reads this value to verify that emitted Windows[].LimitID
137	    // strings are a subset of the documented set, and that the set
138	    // does not regress between binary versions without a deprecation
139	    // cycle. Empty slice is allowed for harnesses that emit no
140	    // windows.
141	    SupportedLimitIDs() []string
142	}
143	
144	// AccountHarness is implemented by harnesses that expose authentication
145	// or account state independent of quota. gemini implements this so its
146	// AuthSnapshot can refresh on its own cadence (7-day freshness window vs.
147	// quota's 15-minute window). claude and codex embed account in their
148	// quota probe and MAY satisfy AccountHarness by re-projecting from
149	// QuotaStatus.Account; that is allowed but not required.
150	type AccountHarness interface {
151	    Harness
152	
153	    // AccountStatus returns the harness's current account/auth state.
154	    // Cheap; reads cached evidence only. Absence of evidence is
155	    // reported via AccountSnapshot.Authenticated=false and
156	    // .Unauthenticated=false (i.e. unknown) on a valid snapshot; the
157	    // error return is reserved for call failure.
158	    AccountStatus(ctx context.Context, now time.Time) (AccountSnapshot, error)
159	
160	    // RefreshAccount drives the harness's account probe (file read,
161	    // CLI call, OAuth state lookup) and persists the result.
162	    // Single-flight per harness instance via the harness's account
163	    // cache lock; concurrent callers block. Probe failure is reported
164	    // via AccountSnapshot fields, not as an error.
165	    RefreshAccount(ctx context.Context) (AccountSnapshot, error)
166	
167	    // AccountFreshness returns the harness's account freshness window
168	    // (e.g. 7 days for gemini, 15 minutes for harnesses whose account
169	    // refreshes coupled with quota). Constant for the harness; cheap.
170	    // Used by the service refresh scheduler to decide when account
171	    // state is stale independent of quota state.
172	    AccountFreshness() time.Duration
173	}
174	
175	// ModelDiscoveryHarness is implemented by harnesses whose model surface
176	// extends beyond a single Info().DefaultModel — i.e. they support family
177	// aliases (sonnet, gpt, gemini) that resolve through discovery evidence.
178	// All five harnesses implement this in the current registry.
179	type ModelDiscoveryHarness interface {
180	    Harness
181	
182	    // DefaultModelSnapshot returns the harness's seed/fallback discovery
183	    // snapshot. Used to bootstrap the catalog before the first live
184	    // refresh lands. Stable for the harness; cheap.
185	    DefaultModelSnapshot() ModelDiscoverySnapshot
186	
187	    // ResolveModelAlias maps a family-style requested model (e.g.
188	    // "sonnet", "gpt", "gemini") to a concrete model ID using the
189	    // provided discovery snapshot. Returns ErrAliasNotResolvable if the
190	    // family is not recognized or the snapshot has no matching concrete
191	    // model.
192	    ResolveModelAlias(family string, snapshot ModelDiscoverySnapshot) (string, error)
193	
194	    // SupportedAliases returns the harness's stable set of family
195	    // aliases ResolveModelAlias recognizes (e.g. "sonnet", "opus",
196	    // "haiku" for claude). Constant for the harness; the harness's
197	    // package doc also enumerates this set for human readers. The
198	    // conformance suite uses this value to verify ResolveModelAlias
199	    // covers each documented family (positive path) and rejects
200	    // out-of-set families with ErrAliasNotResolvable (negative path).
201	    // Empty slice is allowed for harnesses that recognize no family
202	    // aliases (e.g. opencode, pi).
203	    SupportedAliases() []string
204	}
205	```
206	
207	## Universal Types
208

211

213	// status assembly, and operator surfaces. Each harness's private snapshot
214	// type projects into this; the private snapshot is never exposed.
215	type QuotaStatus struct {
216	    // Source identifies how the underlying evidence was captured:
217	    // "pty", "cache", "session-token-count", "cli", "api".
218	    Source string
219	
220	    // CapturedAt is when the underlying evidence was observed (not when
221	    // this status struct was assembled).
222	    CapturedAt time.Time
223	
224	    // Fresh reports whether CapturedAt is within QuotaFreshness() at the
225	    // time of the call.
226	    Fresh bool

229	    Age time.Duration
230

232	    // routing-usable signal; others MUST NOT result in
233	    // RoutingPreferenceAvailable.
234	    State QuotaStateValue
235	
236	    // Windows captures per-window evidence (5h, weekly, tier-specific).
237	    // Authoritative for any structured fact the routing layer or

320	
321	`QuotaWindow`, `ModelDiscoverySnapshot`, `Event`, `ExecuteRequest`,
322	`HarnessInfo`, and `EventType` retain their existing definitions in
323	`internal/harnesses/types.go`. CONTRACT-004 does not redefine them.
324	
325	## Cache and Refresh Ownership
326	
327	Each `QuotaHarness` and `AccountHarness` implementation owns:
328	
329	- The on-disk cache path (defaulted to `$XDG_STATE_HOME/<harness>/quota.json`
330	  or `$XDG_STATE_HOME/<harness>/account.json`; harness-specific paths are
331	  acceptable when documented).
332	- The cache schema, including version bumps and migration.
333	- The freshness window returned by `QuotaFreshness()`.
334	- The probe transport (PTY, CLI, file read).
335	- Lock coordination per ADR-012 (per-source on-disk cache).
336	
337	Service-side code MUST NOT read, write, or compute paths for these caches
338	directly. Operator-visible cache paths SHOULD be exposed through a single
339	service-level diagnostic surface (e.g. `Service.DiagnosticPaths()`) that
340	calls back into each harness via a documented method, rather than the
341	service importing per-harness path functions.
342	
343	## Projection to CONTRACT-003
344	
345	The service layer projects harness-level types onto CONTRACT-003 public
346	types as follows. The projection is the only place service code converts
347	between layers; nothing else in the service may read fields off
348	`QuotaStatus` and re-emit them under different names.
349	
350	| CONTRACT-003 public field | CONTRACT-004 source |
351	|---------------------------|---------------------|
352	| `service.QuotaState.Windows` | `QuotaStatus.Windows` |
353	| `service.QuotaState.CapturedAt` | `QuotaStatus.CapturedAt` |
354	| `service.QuotaState.Fresh` | `QuotaStatus.Fresh` |
355	| `service.QuotaState.Source` | `QuotaStatus.Source` |
356	| `service.QuotaState.Status` | `QuotaStatus.State` (string-cast); when `QuotaStatus.Reason` is non-empty the projection appends ` (<reason>)` so operator surfaces preserve the explanation regardless of `State` value |
357	| `service.QuotaState.LastError` | Non-nil only when the harness method itself returned an error (call failure). State-driven absence (`State=QuotaUnavailable` etc.) does not populate `LastError`; it populates `Status`. |
358	| `service.AccountStatus.Authenticated` | `AccountSnapshot.Authenticated` |
359	| `service.AccountStatus.Unauthenticated` | `AccountSnapshot.Unauthenticated` |
360	| `service.AccountStatus.Email` | `AccountSnapshot.Email` |
361	| `service.AccountStatus.PlanType` | `AccountSnapshot.PlanType` |
362	| `service.AccountStatus.OrgName` | `AccountSnapshot.OrgName` |
363	| `service.AccountStatus.Source` | `AccountSnapshot.Source` |
364	| `service.AccountStatus.CapturedAt` | `AccountSnapshot.CapturedAt` |
365	| `service.AccountStatus.Fresh` | `AccountSnapshot.Fresh` |
366	| `service.AccountStatus.Detail` | `AccountSnapshot.Detail` |
367	| `service.HarnessInfo.Quota` | Result of `QuotaHarness.QuotaStatus()` projected via the rows above; nil when the harness does not implement `QuotaHarness` |
368	| `service.HarnessInfo.Account` | Result of `AccountHarness.AccountStatus()` projected; nil when the harness does not implement `AccountHarness` |
369	| `service.ProviderInfo.Quota` | Same projection as `HarnessInfo.Quota` for subscription-backed providers |
370	| `service.ProviderInfo.Auth` | Same projection as `HarnessInfo.Account` |
371	
372	Routing decisions inside the service consume `QuotaStatus.RoutingPreference`
373	directly. The service MUST NOT project `RoutingPreference` into the public
374	contract; it remains an internal routing signal.
375	
376	`HarnessInfo.Quota` and `HarnessInfo.Account` projections MUST remain
377	backwards-compatible in JSON shape with the current CONTRACT-003 schema.
378	Adding fields is allowed; removing or renaming existing public fields
379	requires a CONTRACT-003 amendment.
380	
381	## Conformance Evidence
382	
383	Every harness implementation MUST carry, at minimum:
384	
385	| Capability | Evidence |
386	|------------|----------|
387	| `Harness` (all) | Unit tests for `Info()`, `HealthCheck()`, and an `Execute` happy path with a final event of correct shape. |
388	| `QuotaHarness` (claude, codex, gemini) | Unit tests for `QuotaStatus` against a synthetic cache fixture (fresh and stale cases); unit tests for `RefreshQuota` against a recorded cassette per ADR-002; unit test asserting `QuotaFreshness` is the documented constant; conformance assertion that every emitted `Windows[].LimitID` value is a member of `SupportedLimitIDs()`. |
389	| `AccountHarness` (gemini; optional for claude/codex) | Unit tests for `AccountStatus` returning each documented state (Authenticated, Unauthenticated, no evidence) with correct `Fresh` against a synthetic file fixture. |
390	| `ModelDiscoveryHarness` (all five) | Unit tests for `DefaultModelSnapshot` returning a non-empty model list. Conformance assertion that `ResolveModelAlias` resolves every family returned by `SupportedAliases()` (positive path) and returns `ErrAliasNotResolvable` for an out-of-set family (negative path). The package documentation MUST enumerate the same set returned by `SupportedAliases()` for human readers; the conformance check is programmatic against `SupportedAliases()`. Stability rule: additive aliases are safe; removing or renaming an alias goes through a deprecation cycle (same as `Windows[].LimitID`). |
391	| Projection | Service-level test asserting CONTRACT-003 JSON shape (e.g. `HarnessInfo.Quota`) is identical before and after the harness migration — pinned to a recorded fixture. |
392	
393	## Invariants
394	
395	These invariants are enforced by reviewer attention and (where practical)
396	by `go vet`-shaped tooling:
397	
398	1. **No service-side import of `internal/harnesses/<name>` symbols beyond
399	   `Runner{}` construction.** Permitted imports from outside the harness's
400	   own package and `internal/harnesses` itself: the runner constructor
401	   used by `internal/serviceimpl/execute_dispatch.go`. All other
402	   consumption MUST go through the interface methods on `Harness`,
403	   `QuotaHarness`, `AccountHarness`, or `ModelDiscoveryHarness`.
404	2. **Cache I/O is harness-owned.** No code outside
405	   `internal/harnesses/<name>/` reads or writes the harness's cache
406	   files.
407	3. **Concrete snapshot types are package-private.** The per-harness
408	   types that today are exported as `ClaudeQuotaSnapshot`,
409	   `CodexQuotaSnapshot`, `GeminiQuotaSnapshot`, etc., become lowercase
410	   (package-private). The interface contract returns `QuotaStatus`.
411	4. **Service routing reads only `RoutingPreference`, `State`, and
412	   `Windows`.** No service-side scoring rule may branch on `Detail`
413	   map contents or internal fields of the per-harness snapshot.
414	   `Windows` is the authoritative structured surface for tier or
415	   per-window facts.
416	5. **No cross-harness imports.** `claude` does not import `codex`;
417	   `claude-tui` (when introduced) does not import `claude`; etc. Shared
418	   helpers go in a neutral package (e.g.
419	   `internal/harnesses/anthropic/`).
420	6. **Runners are stateless wrappers around harness-owned cache files.**
421	   The `Runner` struct MAY hold immutable configuration (binary path,
422	   discovery cache pointer) and lock handles; it MUST NOT hold mutable
423	   quota or account state that would diverge across instances. Two
424	   `&Runner{}` instances of the same harness MUST observe identical
425	   `QuotaStatus`/`AccountSnapshot` results for the same cache state.
426	   The service MAY hold a single registered instance per harness or
427	   construct fresh instances per call; both patterns MUST produce
428	   equivalent observable behavior.
429	7. **`RefreshQuota` and `RefreshAccount` are single-flight per harness
430	   instance, mediated by the harness's on-disk cache lock (ADR-012).**
431	   Concurrent callers block on the lock; once the in-flight refresh
432	   completes, queued callers observe the new cached state. Probe
433	   failure surfaces in the returned status's `State` field, not as an
434	   error return.
435	8. **Errors are reserved for call failure.** `QuotaStatus`,
436	   `RefreshQuota`, `AccountStatus`, and `RefreshAccount` return an
437	   error ONLY for context cancellation, lock acquisition failure, or
438	   unrecoverable IO error. Absence of evidence, missing auth, blocked
439	   quota, and probe failures are reported as state on a valid
440	   returned value. This makes consumer code uniform across success and
441	   failure surfaces.
442	
443	## Non-Goals
444	
445	- Refactoring CONTRACT-003 public types. Their shapes stay the same;
446	  only their *source* changes from per-harness exports to interface
447	  projection.
448	- Generalizing the `HarnessConfig` registry struct. Subprocess-specific
449	  fields (`BaseArgs`, `PermissionArgs`, `ModelFlag`) remain on the
450	  registry struct; they are configuration, not part of the interface.
451	- Introducing a plugin loader or out-of-tree harness support. Harnesses
452	  remain in-tree under `internal/harnesses/`.
453	- Promoting `claude-tui` to a primary harness identity. ADR-013 is
454	  withdrawn pending this contract; re-proposal is a follow-up spec.
455	
456	## Acceptance Criteria
457	
458	1. `internal/harnesses/types.go` declares `Harness`, `QuotaHarness`,
459	   `AccountHarness`, and `ModelDiscoveryHarness` interfaces with the
460	   signatures above.
461	2. `QuotaStatus`, `AccountSnapshot`, `QuotaStateValue`,
462	   `RoutingPreference`, and the three sentinel errors are declared in
463	   `internal/harnesses/types.go`.
464	3. Every existing harness implementation in `internal/harnesses/<name>/`
465	   satisfies `Harness` plus the documented optional sub-interfaces.
466	4. No `.go` file outside `internal/harnesses/` imports a symbol from
467	   `internal/harnesses/claude`, `internal/harnesses/codex`,
468	   `internal/harnesses/gemini`, `internal/harnesses/opencode`, or
469	   `internal/harnesses/pi` other than the runner constructor used by
470	   `internal/serviceimpl/execute_dispatch.go`. A linter check enforces
471	   this.
472	5. Public CONTRACT-003 JSON shapes for `HarnessInfo`, `ProviderInfo`,
473	   `QuotaState`, and `AccountStatus` are byte-identical (modulo
474	   intentionally added fields) to pre-refactor fixtures.
475	6. Per-harness `*QuotaSnapshot` types are lowercased (package-private).
476	7. Per-harness `*QuotaRoutingDecision` types are removed in favor of
477	   `QuotaStatus.RoutingPreference`.
478	8. Per-harness cache I/O functions (`Read*Quota`, `Write*Quota`,
479	   `*QuotaCachePath`) are unexported.
480	9. Conformance evidence (above) lands for every existing harness.
481	10. CONTRACT-003 cross-references CONTRACT-004 as the source for
482	    `HarnessInfo.Quota` and `HarnessInfo.Account` population.
483	
484	## References
485	
486	- [CONTRACT-003 Fizeau Service Interface](./CONTRACT-003-fizeau-service.md)
487	- [ADR-002 PTY Cassette Transport](../adr/ADR-002-pty-cassette-transport.md)
488	- [ADR-011 Cost-Based Routing With Quota Pools](../adr/ADR-011-cost-based-routing-with-quota-pools.md)
489	- [ADR-012 Per-Source On-Disk Cache](../adr/ADR-012-per-source-on-disk-cache.md)
490	- [ADR-014 Universal Harness Interface](../adr/ADR-014-universal-harness-interface.md)
491	- [Primary Harness Capability Baseline](../primary-harness-capability-baseline.md)
492	- [Implementation plan: harness interface refactor](../plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md)

34	// TestSyntheticQuotaHarnessProbeCountSingleFlight verifies that
35	// concurrent RefreshQuota callers share one underlying probe. This is
36	// the synthetic-specific assertion the shared conformance suite does
37	// NOT make.
38	func TestSyntheticQuotaHarnessProbeCountSingleFlight(t *testing.T) {
39		status := harnesses.QuotaStatus{
40			Source:     "synthetic",
41			CapturedAt: time.Now(),
42			Fresh:      true,
43			State:      harnesses.QuotaOK,
44		}
45		h := harnesstest.NewSyntheticQuotaHarness("synthetic-quota-probe", status, nil)
46	
47		// Install a latch so every caller's probe blocks inside the
48		// single-flight cohort until we release it, guaranteeing they
49		// observe each other as in-flight.
50		latch := make(chan struct{})
51		h.SetProbeLatch(latch)
52	
53		const N = 16
54		var (
55			wg    sync.WaitGroup
56			start = make(chan struct{})
57			ready = make(chan struct{}, N)
58		)
59		wg.Add(N)
60		for i := 0; i < N; i++ {
61			go func() {
62				defer wg.Done()
63				ready <- struct{}{}
64				<-start
65				if _, err := h.RefreshQuota(context.Background()); err != nil {
66					t.Errorf("RefreshQuota: %v", err)
67				}
68			}()
69		}
70		for i := 0; i < N; i++ {
71			<-ready
72		}
73		close(start)
74		// Give the goroutines a beat to all enter singleflight.Do before
75		// releasing the latch. A short sleep is acceptable because the
76		// alternative — busy-waiting on ProbeCount — races with the
77		// cohort joining the in-flight call.
78		time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
79		close(latch)
80		wg.Wait()
81	
82		probes := h.ProbeCount()
83		if probes != 1 {
84			t.Errorf("ProbeCount() = %d for %d concurrent RefreshQuota callers; want exactly 1", probes, N)
85		}
86	}

Committed. Summary of changes:

- **`internal/harnesses/claude/contract004.go`** — adds `QuotaStatus`, `RefreshQuota`, `QuotaFreshness`, `SupportedLimitIDs`, `AccountStatus`, `RefreshAccount`, `AccountFreshness`, `DefaultModelSnapshot`, `ResolveModelAlias`, `SupportedAliases` on `*Runner`. `RefreshQuota` probes unconditionally via `captureClaudeQuotaViaPTY` and serializes via a package-scoped `singleflight.Group` so queued callers consume the in-flight result. `mapClaudeRoutingPreference` translates `ClaudeQuotaRoutingDecision` (PreferClaude+freshness) into `(RoutingPreference, QuotaStateValue)`. Probe failures are folded into `State=QuotaUnavailable` (or `QuotaUnauthenticated` for binary-not-found) per CONTRACT-004 invariant #8.
- **`internal/harnesses/claude/doc.go`** — package doc enumerating `SupportedLimitIDs()` (`session`, `weekly-all`, `weekly-sonnet`, `extra`) and `SupportedAliases()` (`sonnet`, `opus`, `haiku`).
- **`internal/harnesses/claude/contract004_conformance_test.go`** — runs `harnesstest.RunHarnessConformance`, `RunQuotaHarnessConformance`, `RunAccountHarnessConformance`, `RunModelDiscoveryHarnessConformance` against `&claude.Runner{}` with an isolated env (empty PATH, temp cache file).

Old per-harness cache I/O (`ReadClaudeQuota`, `WriteClaudeQuota`, `ClaudeQuotaCachePath`, `DecideClaudeQuotaRouting`, `RefreshClaudeQuotaAsync`, etc.) untouched — both surfaces coexist as required. Full `go test ./...` and `golangci-lint ./internal/harnesses/claude/...` pass.
